prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_1> [The Narrator]: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin, hollow cheeks, dark disordered medium-length hair, bright intense dark eyes, thin tense mouth; plain dark wool waistcoat over white shirtsleeves, dark trousers; period clothing. Visual lock: Same lean pale face, dark disordered hair, and bright intense dark eyes in every scene; always plain dark waistcoat and shirtsleeves as default; never elderly, never white-haired, never the filmed blue eye. Voice: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O. Match appearance of reference <IMAGE_1> exactly.

CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "And now at the dead hour of the night, amid the dreadful silence of that". Start speaking immediately with "And" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Ambient bed: louder heartbeat. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

CONTEXT (prior clip in scene — new cast plate refs attached; match location/lighting if still valid; identity from CHARACTER VARIABLES + locked plates only):
INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. Louder. THE NARRATOR's free hand trembles, then stills. Character_The_Narrator still wears loose dark nightshirt, barefoot, holding lantern Camera directive: Close-up on Narrator’s free hand trembling then stilling, heartbeat now thunderous in the silence. Performance: Acting intensity 9/10: Extreme tension face, cheek tic from tremor, then steely eye stillness Optics: f/2.0 shallow depth of field Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, crushed charcoal shadows, cold blue-black tones, single sickly amber lantern ray

Follow the camera framing and location in this prompt exactly. Prioritize the PRIMARY subject and ONE clear action with visible motion; background characters may stay mostly still.

THIS CLIP:
End cleanly when the spoken line and primary action finish — do not hold a frozen pose or empty silence after dialogue.
INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. Louder. THE NARRATOR's free hand trembles, then stills. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_1> says "And now at the dead hour of the night, amid the dreadful silence of that". Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_1> still wears loose dark nightshirt, barefoot, holding lantern Camera directive: Medium shot of Narrator rigid in the dead hour, dreadful house silence broken only by the strange noise. Performance: Acting intensity 9/10: Darting night-terror eyes, pale drawn face, mouth tight on hysteria Optics: f/2.0 shallow depth of field, night dread Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, crushed charcoal shadows, cold blue-black tones, single sickly amber lantern ray / 480p, 24fps

NEGATIVE: no legible text, no watermarks, no logos, no extra limbs, blur/obscure environmental signage or screens, no name tags, no name badges, no embroidered names, no lower thirds, no personal names on clothing or props, no extra unmentioned hats or jackets, no modern wristwatches, no electric light bulbs, no plastic, no zippers,
